description_long: This is a stone watchtower with a square base and a prominent wooden balcony encircling the upper level. The structure features a steep, red-tiled hip roof supported by wooden beams, providing shelter to the balcony area. The lower section is constructed from gray stone blocks, giving it a robust and fortified appearance, while the upper section is defined by wooden railings and supports. A wooden door is visible at the base, accessible by a vertical ladder, suggesting restricted entry. The balcony is enclosed by decorative wooden railings with diagonal cross-bracing, and the roof overhangs to protect the upper platform. The overall design is reminiscent of medieval or Romanesque military architecture, suitable for surveillance or defense in an urban or park setting.
description: A stone and wood watchtower with a red-tiled roof, encircling balcony, and ladder-accessed entrance. The structure features decorative wooden railings and a fortified stone base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden door at the base, vertical ladder, and full balcony with decorative railings.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the stone wall, balcony, and a wooden support beam extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain stone wall, balcony, and no visible entrance or ladder.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with balcony, stone wall, and another wooden support beam visible.
